message.content: how would I pitch brain to a venture capitalist through an instagram dm? he reads them all
how would I pitch brain to a venture capitalist through an instagram dm? he reads them all and will invite 1-2 people to pitch directly to him livestream every week

content.thinking:
The user wants help crafting an Instagram DM pitch to a VC who reads all DMs and invites 1-2 people weekly to pitch on livestream. This is a marketing/copywriting task, not a code task. Let me think about what makes Brain compelling and craft a concise, punchy DM.

Key constraints of Instagram DMs:
- Short attention span
- Needs a hook in the first line
- Must be scannable
- Should create curiosity, not explain everything
- End with a clear ask or CTA

Let me think about Brain's pitch from a VC perspective:

**The problem**: Every company adopting AI agents hits the same wall — agents can't share context, humans become copy-paste secretaries between them, and there's zero governance or auditability.


**The solution**: Brain is the operating system layer for autonomous organizations — a self-correcting knowledge graph that gives AI agents shared memory, governed autonomy, and verifiable intent chains.
**Why now**: Enterprise AI agent adoption is exploding but coordination infrastructure doesn't exist yet. It's like the internet before DNS.
**Market**: Every company running multiple AI agents (which is rapidly becoming every company).
**Differentiation**: Not another agent framework or chatbot. Infrastructure layer — the "operating system" between agents and the org.

Now I'm drafting the actual DM message that hits these points while staying short and scannable.

content.text:
Here's a DM structure optimized for the format — hook, problem, solution, proof, ask:

---

**Option A: Problem-led (recommended)**

> Hey [name] — building something I think fits your thesis on [AI/infra/whatever he talks about].
>
> Every company adopting AI agents hits the same wall: the agents can't talk to each other. Engineers spend 80% of their time as highly-paid secretaries copy-pasting context between Cursor, ChatGPT, and Copilot.
>
> Brain is the shared memory layer for AI agents — a knowledge graph that gives them coordination, governance, and audit trails. Think of it as the operating system between the agents and the org.
>
> Open source, already working, with MCP protocol support so it plugs into existing tools without replacing them.
>
> Would love 5 min on your livestream to show it live. Happy to demo agents coordinating in real-time — it clicks when you see it.

---

**Option B: Shorter/punchier**

> Hey [name] — quick one. I built the missing infrastructure layer for AI agents.
>
> Right now every AI agent has amnesia. No shared memory, no governance, no audit trail. Humans are the integration bus.
>
> Brain fixes that — shared knowledge graph, governed autonomy, verifiable intent chains. Open source. Works with every major coding agent today via MCP.
>
> It's a 2-minute demo that sells itself. Would love to show it on your livestream.

---

**Tips for this specific format:**
- Lead with something that shows you've watched his content ("fits your thesis on X")
- "Demo" is the magic word — it implies you have something real, not a deck
- "Open source" signals conviction and traction potential
- Don't attach links in the first DM — Instagram deprioritizes DMs with URLs
- If he responds, *then* drop the GitHub link and a 60-second Loom

Want me to tailor either version to his specific investment focus?

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

message.content: the vision is "as being the foundational ai native business os that will create the next generation of unicorn companies by a single person"

content.text:
That changes the pitch significantly — you're not selling dev tooling, you're selling a platform thesis: **one person + Brain = unicorn**.

---

**Recommended DM:**

> Hey [name] — building the AI-native business OS. The thesis is simple: the next wave of unicorns will be built by solo founders, but only if agents can actually run a company — not just answer questions.
>
> Right now AI agents have no shared memory, no governance, no accountability. You can't hand them real authority because there's no operating system underneath.
>
> Brain is that OS. Agents coordinate through a knowledge graph, every action has an auditable intent chain, and trust is earned incrementally — not granted blindly. One founder can run strategy, engineering, ops, and compliance through agents that actually talk to each other.
>
> Open source, working today, built on the MCP protocol so it's not locked to any model vendor.
>
> I'd love to demo it on your livestream — a single person orchestrating an autonomous org in real-time. It's the kind of thing that clicks when you see it.

---

**Why this works for a VC:**
- "Next generation of unicorns" is a fund-returning narrative, not a feature pitch
- "Solo founder" signals capital efficiency (their favorite word)
- "Open source" implies community moat + ecosystem play
- The livestream demo offer is compelling — watching one person run an org through agents is inherently dramatic content
- No jargon about knowledge graphs or SurrealDB — that's for the follow-up

**One-liner if he asks "what is it" in reply:**

> The operating system that lets one person run a billion-dollar company through AI agents — with the governance to actually trust them.
